reasoning: The reference value of 128.25 protests per month was established during a period of intense, widespread weekly demonstrations across dozens of Israeli intersections, primarily driven by the Hostage Families Forum and anti-government movements demanding a ceasefire and hostage deal. However, search results confirm that on October 13, 2025, a ceasefire agreement successfully secured the release of the remaining 20 living hostages (search_4_result_2) (search_4_result_9). The resolution of the main crisis that galvanized routine nationwide, decentralized protests will likely cause the overall volume of protest events (as coded by ACLED) to drop significantly. While some protests will undoubtedly continue—such as right-wing backlash over the release of Palestinian prisoners or the opposition's pivot to demanding early elections—they are unlikely to consistently span the 80+ distinct geographical locations required to surpass the highly inflated baseline of 128.25 events per 30 days. As the forecast horizon extends into late 2025 and 2026, the potential for new, unpredictable political catalysts (e.g., early elections) increases uncertainty, pushing probabilities progressively closer to 0.5, as required.
